title: Tokenisation via Convex Relaxations
abstract_en: Tokenisation is an integral part of the current NLP pipeline. Current tokenisation algorithms such as BPE and Unigram are greedy algorithms -- they make locally optimal decisions without considering the resulting vocabulary as a whole. We instead formulate tokeniser construction as a linear program and solve it using convex optimisation tools, yielding a new algorithm we call ConvexTok. We find ConvexTok consistently improves intrinsic tokenisation metrics and the bits-per-byte (BpB) achieved by language models; it also improves downstream task performance, but less consistently. Furthermore, ConvexTok allows the user to certify how far their tokeniser is from optimal, with respect to a certain objective, via a lower bound, and we empirically find it to be within 1\% of optimal at common vocabulary sizes.
summary_cn: 当前主流的分词算法如BPE和Unigram是贪心算法，仅做局部最优决策。本文将其构建为线性规划问题，并借助凸优化工具求解，提出新算法ConvexTok。实验表明，ConvexTok在内在分词指标、语言模型的每字节比特数上持续提升，在下游任务上也有一定改进，且能通过下界证明分词器距离最优解通常不超过1%。
one_sentence: 本文提出将分词器构建问题形式化为线性规划，并利用凸优化工具求解，从而得到一种全局最优而非局部贪心的新分词算法ConvexTok。

title: Which Way Did It Move? Diagnosing and Overcoming Directional Motion Blindness in Video-LLMs
abstract_en: Video Large Language Models (Video-LLMs) have made rapid progress on temporal video understanding, yet many fail at a basic perceptual primitive: signed image-plane motion direction. On simple videos of a single object moving left, right, up, or down, most Video-LLMs perform near chance, with above-chance cases largely attributable to prediction biases rather than genuine direction understanding. We call this failure directional motion blindness. We localize the failure by tracing motion direction information through the Video-LLM pipeline. Motion direction remains linearly accessible from the vision encoder, projector, and LLM hidden states, but the readout fails to bind this signal to the correct verbal answer option, revealing a direction binding gap. Although synthetic motion direction instruction tuning reduces this gap on the source domain, motion direction concept vector analysis shows that visual complexity weakens the signal magnitude and limits out-of-domain generalization. We introduce MoDirect, a dataset family for motion direction instruction tuning and evaluation, and DeltaDirect, a diagnosis-driven, projector-level objective that predicts normalized 2-D motion vectors from adjacent-frame feature deltas. On MoDirect-SynBench, instruction tuning with DeltaDirect improves motion direction accuracy from 25.9% to 85.4%. On MoDirect-RealBench, DeltaDirect improves real-world motion direction accuracy by 21.9 points over the vanilla baseline without real-world tuning data, while preserving standard video-understanding performance. Code: https://github.com/KHU-VLL/DeltaDirect
summary_cn: 该论文发现大多数视频大语言模型在判断简单对象运动方向（左、右、上、下）时表现接近随机，称为方向运动盲视。尽管运动方向信息在视觉编码器、投影层和大语言模型隐藏状态中线性可访问，但模型无法将其绑定到正确回答选项，揭示了方向绑定鸿沟。为此，作者提出了MoDirect数据集和DeltaDirect方法，通过投影层预测相邻帧特征差异的归一化二维运动向量，在合成基准上将准确率从25.9%提升至85.4%，在真实基准上提升21.9个百分点，同时保持标准视频理解性能。
one_sentence: 本文提出DeltaDirect，一种基于投影层和相邻帧特征差异来预测归一化二维运动向量的诊断驱动目标，以解决视频大语言模型在基本平面运动方向感知上的方向绑定失效问题。

title: Integrable Elasticity via Neural Demand Potentials
abstract_en: We propose the Integrable Context-Dependent Demand Network (ICDN), a demand-first neural model for multiproduct retail demand. The model learns log-demand as a smooth, context-conditioned function of log-prices, allowing elasticities to be derived exactly from the learned demand surface. On the Dominick's beer dataset, ICDN improves out-of-sample generalization over a directed log-log benchmark and yields more stable, economically plausible elasticity estimates, especially for weakly identified cross-price effects.
summary_cn: 本文提出了一种名为可积分的上下文依赖需求网络（ICDN）的需求优先神经网络模型，用于多产品零售需求预测。该模型将对数需求学习为关于对数价格的光滑、上下文条件函数，从而能够从学习到的需求曲面中精确推导出弹性。在Dominick's啤酒数据集上的实验表明，ICDN相比有向对数对数基准模型提升了样本外泛化性能，并生成了更稳定、经济上合理的弹性估计，尤其在弱识别的交叉价格效应方面表现突出。
one_sentence: 本文提出了一种可积分的上下文依赖需求网络（ICDN），通过将对数需求建模为对数价格的光滑上下文条件函数，从而直接从学习到的需求曲面中精确推导出弹性。

title: Cambrian-P: Pose-Grounded Video Understanding
abstract_en: Camera pose matters. The position and orientation of each viewpoint define a shared spatial coordinate frame that relates observations across video frames. Yet this signal is largely absent from multimodal LLMs (MLLMs) for video understanding, which process frames as isolated 2D snapshots, instead of the persistent scene humans perceive. We revisit pose as a lightweight supervisory signal and introduce Cambrian-P, a video MLLM augmented with per-frame learnable camera tokens and a pose regression head. With a carefully designed sampling scheme, the model achieves substantial gains of 4.5-6.5% on spatial reasoning benchmarks such as VSI-Bench, generalizes across eight additional spatial and general video QA benchmarks, and, as a byproduct, achieves state of the art streaming pose estimation on ScanNet. Surprisingly, training on pseudo-annotated poses from in-the-wild video further improves general video QA benchmarks, showing pose helps beyond spatial reasoning. Together, these results position camera pose as a fundamental signal for video models that reason about the physical world.
summary_cn: 本文指出相机位姿是视频理解中被忽略的关键信号，并提出了Cambrian-P模型。该模型通过为每帧添加可学习的相机位姿标记和位姿回归头，结合精心设计的采样方案，在空间推理基准上取得4.5-6.5%的显著提升，同时在多个视频问答和流式位姿估计任务上达到最优。实验表明，伪标注位姿也能提升通用视频问答性能，证明位姿超越了纯空间推理的范畴。
one_sentence: 本文提出将相机位姿作为轻量级监督信号，通过注入可学习的位姿标记和回归头来增强视频多模态大模型的空间推理能力。

title: MotiMotion: Motion-Controlled Video Generation with Visual Reasoning
abstract_en: Current motion-controlled image-to-video generation models rigidly follow user-provided trajectories that are often sparse, imprecise, and causally incomplete. Such reliance often yields unnatural or implausible outcomes, especially by missing secondary causal consequences. To address this, we introduce MotiMotion, a novel framework that reformulates motion control as a reasoning-then-generation problem. To encourage causally grounded and commonsense-consistent interactions, we leverage a training-free vision-language reasoner to refine image-space coordinates of primary trajectories and to hallucinate plausible secondary motions. To further improve motion naturalness, we propose a confidence-aware control scheme that modulates guidance strength, enabling the model to closely follow high-confidence plans while correcting artifacts under low-confidence inputs with its internal generative priors. To support systematic evaluation, we curate a new image-to-video benchmark, MotiBench, consisting of interaction-centric scenes where new events are triggered by motion. Both VLM-based evaluation and a human study on MotiBench demonstrate that MotiMotion produces videos with more plausible object behaviors and interaction, and is preferred over existing approaches.
summary_cn: 本文提出MotiMotion框架，解决当前运动控制图像转视频模型因轨迹稀疏、不精确而生成不自然结果的问题。该方法将运动控制视为推理-生成问题，利用无训练视觉语言推理器优化主运动轨迹并推理出合理的次要运动，同时通过置信度感知控制动态调节引导强度，在低置信度区域利用生成先验修正伪影。新提出的MotiBench基准测试表明，该方法在生成合理物体交互行为上优于现有方法。
one_sentence: 本文提出MotiMotion框架，将运动控制重构为推理-生成问题，通过无训练视觉语言推理器优化轨迹并补充因果合理的次要运动，结合置信度感知控制提升生成自然性。
